behaviors.description: Reject plugin with missing required manifest field before executing any plugin code.
behaviors.justification: `missing a required field is rejected before any code ... executed` tests handoff between manifest validation and module import/execution, not just schema logic. A unit test of validator alone can miss a real bug where loader still imports the module. Higher levels rejected: no real network/subprocess/OS-specific primitive is required by behavior text; no AI call.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugin dir with one plugin whose `manifest.json` omits a required field and whose entry module writes a sentinel file (or mutates shared state) on import. Run loader end-to-end in-process. Assert loader reports rejection and sentinel side effect never occurs.

behaviors.description: Dependency order: if A depends on B, call `B.onLoad` before `A.onLoad`.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly targets cross-plugin ordering of hook invocation (`plugin B's onLoad ... before plugin A's`), which is module orchestration, not a single-function assertion. Unit topo-sort test is insufficient because invocation plumbing can fail. Higher levels rejected: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement in text; no AI/ML output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Build two test plugins in a temp directory: A declares dependency on B; each `onLoad` appends its name to shared log. Run loader and assert log order is `[B, A]`.

behaviors.description: Detect mutual dependency cycle and load neither plugin.
behaviors.justification: `reports the cycle and loads neither` requires interaction between cycle detection and load/execution suppression across plugins. A unit cycle-detector test alone can miss real failure where loader still executes hooks. Higher levels rejected: behavior does not require real network/subprocess/OS behavior; no AI/ML calls.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins A and B with mutual dependencies. Add import and `onLoad` side-effect sentinels. Run loader, assert cycle error is reported, and no sentinel from either plugin is produced.

behaviors.description: `onLoad` runs only after plugin entry module import/execution.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text is an ordering contract across module import side effects and lifecycle hook execution (`only invoked after ... imported and executed`). This is a boundary between loader and runtime module execution, beyond single-function logic. Higher levels rejected: can be validated in one Node process without real external infra; no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Plugin entry module sets `globalThis.pluginInitialized = true` during import; `onLoad` asserts that flag is already true and records call. Run loader and assert no assertion failure plus recorded order indicates import-side effect happened before `onLoad`.

behaviors.description: If one plugin throws in `onLoad`, loader continues with remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: This checks failure isolation plus dependency-aware continuation across multiple plugins (`continues initializing remaining plugins that do not depend on it`). That is multi-module orchestration/state handoff, not isolated function logic. Higher levels rejected: no requirement for real network/subprocess/OS primitives; no LLM/AI output in execution path.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins: X throws in `onLoad`; Y depends on X; Z is independent. Run loader, assert X failure is recorded, Y is skipped/not initialized due to dependency, and Z `onLoad` still runs successfully.
